exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title, framed explicitly around two areas of knowledge
Effective use of concrete examples (Stonehenge, Moon landing, SpaceX) to support the central claim
Early signposting and clear thesis statement that guide the reader through the argument
Awareness of contrasting perspectives (knowledge can both diminish and enhance wonder)
Succinct conclusion that restates the dual impact of knowledge on wonder across disciplines
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely narrative and descriptive, with limited exploration of underlying epistemic processes
Evaluation of counterclaims is minimal and not critically examined in depth
Transitions between sections are abrupt, weakening overall coherence
Engagement with methodology and ways of knowing is lacking, reducing analytical depth
Overâreliance on popular sources and occasional citation errors undermine academic rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band: it maintains a focused discussion of the title, links effectively to two areas of knowledge, and supports arguments with relevant examples. However, it falls short of the 7â8 band due to its primarily narrative treatment, limited critical evaluation of counterclaims, and insufficient analytical depth regarding epistemic concepts and methodologies. A mark of 5 appropriately reflects this level of performance.

annotations.comment: The transition to the history AOK is abrupt; a more explicit link back to the central knowledge question would strengthen cohesion between sections.
The Stonehenge example is well chosen to show the progression from magical to archaeological interpretation, supporting the thesis that knowledge can erode wonder.
There is no explicit return to the central question after this historical example; reinforcing the link between Stonehenge and the title would improve structural coherence.
The Moon landing example brings in a significant scientific milestone, effectively illustrating how acquiring frontier knowledge can inspire renewed wonder.
Linking climate change concerns to renewed scientific wonder about space exploration shows strong relevance and connects the historical example to contemporary issues.
The Elon Musk quotation is truncated and lacks full citation detail; ensure direct quotes are accurate and properly referenced to maintain credibility.
Using a high-profile modern example (SpaceX) effectively demonstrates how knowledge acquisition continues to generate wonder in the natural sciences.
There is limited engagement with alternative historical interpretations or counterclaims; exploring scholarly debates would deepen critical evaluation.
The conclusion succinctly restates the dual impact of knowledge on wonder across the two AOKs, reinforcing the central thesis in a clear summary.
Early signposting of the two AOKs provides a clear organizational framework, helping the reader anticipate the progression of the argument.

The student presents a clear thesis and scope by framing the prescribed title and identifying natural sciences and history as the two areas of knowledge to be discussed, setting an explicit focus for the essay.
The introduction does not pose an explicit knowledge question or employ TOK terminology, which would help anchor the discussion in critical inquiry rather than narrative description.
The claim that childrenâs wonder declines with knowledge acquisition is asserted without evidence; consider citing developmental psychology or empirical studies to support this generalisation.
The inclusion of Newtonâs prism experiments effectively demonstrates a scientific paradigm shift, illustrating how explanation replaced myth and diminished original wonder.
The definitions of âwonderâ and âdestroyâ are overly simplistic and lack engagement with epistemological nuance; consider exploring how these concepts function within knowledge frameworks.
The roadmap sentence signposts structure but remains general; consider more precise linking of each example to the central claim to improve coherence.
The treatment of Stonehenge lacks exploration of the epistemic methods (e.g., radiocarbon dating, stratigraphy) that produced the new knowledge, missing an opportunity to deepen the analysis.
The discussion of ancient Greek and Hindu myths vividly illustrates how pre-scientific explanations generated wonder, grounding the argument in concrete cultural examples.
This description remains largely narrative; the student should critically analyse how these myths functioned epistemically and what their eventual displacement reveals about knowledge change.
There is a missed opportunity to examine how advances in technology (rockets, telemetry) and methodology underpin the expansion of wonder in the natural sciences.
The discussion of revised theories about Egyptian pyramid builders (Hawass) is a valuable historical insight that illustrates how new knowledge can renew wonder.

The link between new historical evidence and enhanced wonder is asserted rather than analysed; the student should examine how methodological advances (tomb analysis) reshape historiographical understanding.
Reliance on a single popular source (âOnion, 2023â) weakens academic rigour; the student should engage with peer-reviewed archaeology literature to substantiate claims.
The mention of unresolved mysteries is introduced as a counterclaim but not developed; the student could analyse how remaining gaps maintain wonder and what this implies about knowledge limits.
The discussion of Neil Armstrongâs continued curiosity remains anecdotal; the student should analyse how the scientific method and first-hand observation contribute to sustaining wonder.
While the conclusion summarizes effectively, it misses an opportunity to synthesise the broader implications of the arguments or suggest areas for further enquiry.
The essay lacks reflection on ways of knowing (e.g., emotion, reason) which would enrich the TOK analysis by showing how different WOKs mediate the experience of wonder.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
